acceptance_criteria: expert_memo.md contains corrected O2/R2 margin (75.0%) and a 'changes since baseline' note.
compressor_surge_1.xlsx includes a FORMULAS sheet listing derived-cell formulas that reference embedded inputs.
A CSV or text export of the FORMULAS sheet is attached and parseable.
Memo clarifies remediation scope (per-record vs system-wide) and cost accounting matches spreadsheet values.
Verifier re-run returns no blocking issues (verifier_status == pass or pass_without_warnings).
expected_outcomes: Spreadsheet formulas explicitly visible and exportable
Re-run verifier passes formula-level checks or, if environment still lacks openpyxl, the exported formulas enable verifier to confirm derivations

planned_changes.description: Update expert_memo.md numeric text: correct O2 applied to R2 from 75.6% to 75.0% and review any other percent values for consistency with spreadsheet calculations.

Modify compressor_surge_1.xlsx so derived cells use explicit formulas referencing the embedded input cells. Add a 'FORMULAS' worksheet that lists each derived cell, its source formula, and the cell references it depends on.

Export the 'FORMULAS' sheet to a CSV or plain-text file and include it with the attempt artifacts (e.g., compressor_surge_1_formulas.csv) so automated graders/verifiers can parse formulas even if openpyxl is unavailable.

Add a short section in expert_memo.md explicitly stating whether remediation options are applied per-record or system-wide. If both analyses are relevant, include both with corresponding cost accounting and results.
Re-run all sensitivity checks and budget calculations with the corrected spreadsheet, validate results against memo narrative, and update the memo with any changed numbers or conclusions.

Attach updated compressor_surge_1.xlsx, the formulas export, and the revised expert_memo.md to the revised attempt. In the memo, add a short 'Changes since baseline' appendix describing the fixes and artifacts added.

raw_llm_output.revision_reason: Fix minor arithmetic inconsistency and enable programmatic verification of spreadsheet formulas; clarify remediation application scope and re-run sensitivity checks.

raw_model_output.revision_reason: Fix minor arithmetic inconsistency and enable programmatic verification of spreadsheet formulas; clarify remediation application scope and re-run sensitivity checks.

metadata_json.revision_goal: Baseline deliverables are present and the memo reasoning is sound: recommendation to APPROVE LAUNCH is supported by recomputed surge_margin values and classifications. Two non-blocking issues were identified: (1) a minor arithmetic inconsistency in the memo for O2 applied to R2 (memo reports 75.6% but correct recomputation yields 75.0%), and (2) the grader/verifier could not programmatically inspect spreadsheet formulas because the environment lacked openpyxl, preventing automated verification that derived cells reference embedded inputs. These issues do not change the classification outcome but should be corrected for full credit and reproducibility.
